# Accessibility Checklist: Troubleshooting, Security, Trust, Update, And Rollback

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-008 requirements quality for static Markdown accessibility, semantic dense matrices, stable support links, and browser-safe documentation boundaries.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Does DOC-008 require troubleshooting and update/rollback matrices to use semantic Markdown tables or equivalent sectioned lists with explicit headers for every required field? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-011]
- [x] CHK002 Are troubleshooting and recovery row labels required to be readable outside table position, such as stable row headings, slugs, anchors, or first-column labels that preserve meaning for screen-reader and static Markdown review? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-011, Data Model Troubleshooting Row]
- [x] CHK003 Are all troubleshooting row fields specified so dense support rows cannot omit symptom, likely cause, inspect command or file, recommended fix, platform label, follow-up link, or citation? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec SC-002]
- [x] CHK004 Are all update/rollback recovery fields specified so each case includes checkpoint, manual action, side effect, reload or restart expectation, and source evidence? [Completeness, Spec FR-011, Spec SC-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are non-browser execution boundaries complete enough to prevent docs from running local diagnostics, granting permissions, inspecting local files, invoking plugin workflows, or modifying configuration? [Completeness, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-018]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Are stable heading and link-text requirements explicit enough to support deep links without generic or ambiguous link labels in troubleshooting, security/trust, and update/rollback content? [Clarity, Spec FR-017, Data Model Troubleshooting Row]
- [x] CHK007 Are read-only inspection fields clearly distinguished from mutating recovery fields so dense matrices do not rely on visual placement alone to communicate safety? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec Read-Only Inspection, Spec Manual Recovery Action]
- [x] CHK008 Is platform labeling constrained to `Claude Code`, `Codex`, or `Both` so screen-reader users and static Markdown readers can understand applicability without color, icons, or layout cues? [Clarity, Spec FR-005]
- [x] CHK009 Is the security/trust evidence-type separation clear enough to be represented by headings, labeled groups, or table columns rather than relying on visual proximity? [Clarity, Spec FR-007, Spec SC-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK010 Do troubleshooting, security/trust, and update/rollback requirements consistently avoid interactive-only controls and preserve static Markdown as the complete source of reader guidance? [Consistency, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-018, Plan Documentation Boundaries]
- [x] CHK011 Do DOC-008 accessibility requirements stay within DOC-008 page content instead of taking over DOC-010 site-wide search, responsive, accessibility tooling, or docs CI hardening scope? [Consistency, Spec Assumptions, Workflow Phase 4]
- [x] CHK012 Do matrix accessibility requirements align with the plan's docs-only file operations and avoid new runtime components, browser storage, or diagnostic widgets? [Consistency, Plan Technical Context, Plan Declared File Operations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Is there a measurable success criterion for dense DOC-008 matrices remaining understandable as static Markdown with semantic headers, readable row labels, stable headings, and descriptive link text? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-009]
- [x] CHK014 Are no-browser-execution outcomes measurable through forbidden local execution, permission, workflow, filesystem, and configuration behaviors rather than subjective safety language? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-018]
- [x] CHK015 Are deep-link outcomes measurable through internal link resolution and source/reference link requirements? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec FR-017, Spec SC-001, Spec SC-008]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK016 Are keyboard and screen-reader concerns covered for the dense matrix reading scenario without requiring custom interactive controls? [Coverage, Plan Page Architecture, Workflow Phase 4]
- [x] CHK017 Are static Markdown readers covered for troubleshooting and update/rollback cases where tables are copied into support replies or rendered without browser-side enhancement? [Coverage, Spec US1, Spec US3, Research Decision 2]
- [x] CHK018 Are evaluator readers covered for security/trust sections that must distinguish official vendor behavior, repository facts, and recommended practice without relying on color or visual grouping alone? [Coverage, Spec US2, Spec FR-007, Spec SC-005]
